bird.name: 李爾氏金剛鸚鵡

locations.name: Caatinga
locations.summary: Caatinga (; [kaaˈt͡ʃĩɡɐ]) is a type of semi-arid tropical vegetation, and an ecoregion characterized by this vegetation…

relations.subject: 李爾氏金剛鸚鵡
relations.evidence: It inhabits a dry desert-like shrubby environment known as caatinga, and roosts and nests in cavities in sandstone cliffs

relations.subject: 李爾氏金剛鸚鵡
relations.evidence: right|thumb|Sandstone cliffs in Bahia, Brazil right|thumb|Nests in sandstone cliffs in Bahia, Brazil For a century and a half after it had been described, the species was only known from sporadic occurrences in the bird trade, and the whereabouts of the wild population was unknown. A wild population was eventually discovered in 1978 by ornithologist Helmut Sick in Bahia in the interior northeast of Brazil
relations.object_summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

relations.subject: 李爾氏金剛鸚鵡
relations.evidence: In response to this, tens of thousands of palms were propagated and planted in fenced-off areas in the early 2000s. Lear's macaw also requires pre-existing natural cavities found in the sandstone cliffs in which to nest

relations.evidence: The 'Threats' section was updated to emphasise reduction of food resources due to habitat loss caused by the historical expansion of agricultural development in the region (criterion B1b(iii))
